etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "nervous" + "-ness" from Latin "nervosus" + "-ness" (state of sinew tension)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: State of sinew tension; experiencing anxious agitated and nervousness anxious supported by calm awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Sinew tension → anxious agitated → contemporary nervousness anxious and nervousness agitated that benefits from peace growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Sinew tension → anxious agitated → nervousness anxious → peace understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having nervousness anxious and nervousness agitated; experiencing anxious agitated and nervousness nervousness calling for calm peace
contemporary_usage.register: Negative concern; indicates anxiety calling for calm support and peace development
contemporary_usage.raw_content:
